name: Parallel Heels
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs parallel, heels placed on footbar, feet hip width apart

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Initiate movement with hip extensors
Keep feet dorsiflexed and stable as if standing on floor
Straighten legs completely, avoiding hyperextension
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Parallel Toes
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs parallel, toes placed on footbar, feet hip width apart

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Initiate movement with hip extensors
Establish plantarflexion when legs are straight
Keep heels stable moving around ankle joint
Straighten legs completely, avoiding hyperextension
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: V-Position Toes
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, hips externally rotated, toes placed on footbar, heels together, small V position

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Initiate movement with hip extensors
Engage hip adductors
Establish plantarflexion when legs are straight
Keep heels squeezing together and stable
Straighten legs completely, avoiding hyperextension
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Open V-Position Heels
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, hips externally rotated, heels placed on outside edge of footbar, wide V position

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Initiate movement with hip extensors
Engage hip adductors
Keep feet dorsiflexed and stable as if standing on the floor
Straighten legs completely, avoiding hyperextension
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Open V-Position Toes
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, hips externally rotated, toes placed on outside edge of footbar, wide V position

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Initiate movement with hip extensors
Straighten legs completely, avoiding hyperextension
Engage hip adductors
Establish plantarflexion when legs are straight
Keep heels stable moving around ankle joint
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Calf Raises
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs straight and parallel, toes placed on footbar, feet hip width apart and plantarflexed

movement.action: Lower heels
Lift heels, return to start position

cues: Lower heels with control
Use full range of motion of ankle joint
Maintain straight legs, avoiding hyperextension
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Prances
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs straight and parallel, toes placed on footbar, feet hip width apart and plantarflexed

movement.action: Lower one heel and lift other simultaneously, switch
Lower one heel and lift other simultaneously, switch; final exhale: lift both heels, return to start position

cues: Work both feet equally in full range of motion
Straighten legs and plantarflex feet completely between each repetition
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Prehensile
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs parallel, toes and balls of feet wrapped around footbar (Prehensile position), feet hip width apart, heels pressing down

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Initiate movement with hip extensors
Straighten legs completely
Keep heels pressing down throughout movement
Maintain wrapped position of toes and balls of feet
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Single Leg Heel
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, feet hip width apart, heel of one foot placed on footbar, other leg bent at 90 degree angle in hip and knee joints (tabletop position)

movement.action: Straighten working leg
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Initiate movement with hip extensors
Straighten leg completely, avoiding hyperextension
Keep bent leg stable
Keep foot of working leg dorsiflexed and stable as if standing on floor
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Single Leg Toes
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, feet hip width apart, toes of one foot placed on footbar, other leg bent at 90 degree angle in hip and knee joints (tabletop position)

movement.action: Straighten working leg
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Initiate movement with hip extensors
Straighten leg completely, avoiding hyperextension
Keep bent leg stable
Establish plantarflexion when leg is straight
Keep heel stable
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Hundred Prep
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs in tabletop position, arms straight and perpendicular to floor, hands in straps, palms facing forward

movement.action: Lift head and chest, reach arms down to sides of body, parallel to floor
Lift arms to 90 degrees in the shoulder joint, lower chest and head, return to start position

cues: Move head together with upper girdle
Maintain straight arms aligned with shoulders
Keep eyeline directly forward when in trunk flexion
Keep tension in straps throughout movement

name: Hundred
setup: Starting in Hundred Prep position

movement.action: Lift head and chest, reach arms down to sides of body, parallel to floor, straighten legs forward; pump arms up and down for 5 counts
Pump arms up and down for 5 counts; final: return to start position

cues: Keep pumps of arms small
Keep legs stable at safe height
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Coordination
setup: Starting in Hundred Prep position

movement.action: Lift head and chest, reach arms down to sides of body, parallel to floor, straighten legs forward, open legs, close legs
Bend legs toward chest, lift arms to 90 degrees in the shoulder joint, lower chest and head, return to start position

cues: Keep feet opposite eye line when in trunk flexion
Bend knees toward chest before lifting arms and lowering trunk
Open legs no wider than footbar

name: Round Back
setup: Sitting upright at front of Short Box, knees bent, feet under foot strap, hip width apart, arms crossed and resting on chest
resistance: all springs attached to hold carriage in place

movement.action: Round trunk into C curve, shoulders above hip joints, lift arms parallel to floor, lower trunk back; lift trunk to sitting C curve
Pause; straighten back and lower arms, return to start position

cues: Initiate with trunk flexion prior to lowering trunk back
Maintain C curve position during lowering and lifting phases
In final phase extend trunk when shoulders are above hip joints

name: Flat Back
setup: Starting in Round Back position, extend spine, hands lightly interlaced behind head
resistance: all springs attached to hold carriage in place

movement.action: Hinge trunk back
Hinge trunk forward, return to start position

cues: Avoid thrusting ribs forward
Keep head aligned with spine
Touch fingers loosely behind head
Maintain co-contraction of abdominals and back extensors
Move around hip joints

name: Tilt
setup: Starting in Flat Back position
resistance: all springs attached to hold carriage in place

movement.action: Arc trunk to one side
Lift back to center, return to start position; arc trunk to opposite side

objectives: Spinal lateral flexor control and stretch
cues: Maintain co-contraction of abdominals and back extensors
Keep both sit bones in contact with box as trunk arcs over
Keep elbows wide and head aligned with spine
Touch fingers loosely behind head
Keep pelvis stable

name: Twist
setup: Starting in Flat Back position
resistance: all springs attached to hold carriage in place

movement.action: Rotate trunk, lower trunk diagonally back
Lift trunk to center, rotate to front, return to start position; rotate trunk to opposite side

cues: Maintain co-contraction of abdominals and back extensors
Keep both sit bones in contact with box
Rotate on longitudinal axis prior to hinging diagonally back
Return to longitudinal axis prior to facing front

name: Roundabout
setup: Starting in Flat Back position
resistance: all springs attached to hold carriage in place

movement.action: Rotate trunk, lower trunk diagonally back
Rotate to opposite side, lift trunk to center, rotate to front, return to start position

cues: Maintain co-contraction of abdominals and back extensors
Keep both sit bones in contact with box
Rotate on longitudinal axis prior to hinging diagonally back
Return to longitudinal axis prior to facing front

name: Climb-a-Tree
setup: Sitting upright at front of Long Box, one foot under foot strap, other leg bent with thigh to chest, holding leg below knee, hands on shin
resistance: all springs attached to hold carriage in place

movement.action: Pump knee to chest 3 times; round back, walk hands down leg 3 times beginning with outside hand, lifting leg to perpendicular to floor; walk hands up leg 3 times beginning with inside hand
Straighten leg holding above ankle; arch back over box, circle arms overhead and around, lift head and chest, hold onto leg; straighten spine, bend leg, return to start position

cues: Keep leg perpendicular to floor as arms circle around
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization as arms circle around

name: Frog
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, hips externally rotated, feet in straps, dorsiflexed, heels together, hips and knees at 90 degrees

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Keep squeezing heels together
Avoid bending knees beyond 90 degrees
Keep heels traveling along horizontal line
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Circles Down
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs straight, perpendicular to floor, hips externally rotated, feet in straps, plantarflexed

movement.action: Lower legs
Open legs and circle around, lift legs, return to start position

cues: Focus on hip disassociation
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Draw straight line down center, squeezing heels together
Draw two semi-circles back to back as legs circle around
Maintain consistent flow of movement

name: Circles Up
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs straight, perpendicular to floor, hips externally rotated, feet in straps, plantarflexed

movement.action: Open legs out to sides, circle legs around and together
Lift legs, return to start position

cues: Focus on hip disassociation
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Draw straight line up center, squeezing heels together
Draw two semi-circles back to back as legs circle around
Maintain consistent flow of movement

name: Openings
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs straight on diagonal line (45 to 60 degrees), hips externally rotated, feet in straps, plantarflexed

movement.action: Open legs
Close legs, return to start position

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Keep feet moving at consistent height from floor as legs open and close
Move within the limits of pelvic lumbar control

name: Frog Extended
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, hips externally rotated, feet in straps, dorsiflexed, heels together, hips and knees at 80 degrees

movement.action: Straighten legs; bend legs, bring heels together, return to start position
Open legs; pause

cues: Keep heels squeezing together as legs straighten
Keep carriage still as legs bend in from open position
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Frog Extended Reverse
setup: Starting in Frog Extended position

movement.action: Straighten legs out to sides; close legs
Pause; bend legs, return to start position

cues: Keep heels squeezing together as legs bend
Keep carriage still as legs open out to sides
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Bottom Lift
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs parallel, feet hip width apart, toes placed on footbar, knees bent, arms by sides, headrest down

movement.action: Roll spine up; roll spine down
Pause; pause

cues: Feet position is same as Parallel Toes in Footwork
Keep heels still, adductors engaged and legs parallel
Minimize movement of carriage when rolling spine up and down
Articulate through each vertebra as spine rolls up and down

name: Bottom Lift with Extension
setup: Starting in Bottom Lift position

movement.action: Roll spine up; straighten legs (not completely); roll spine down, return to start position
Pause; bend legs

cues: Feet position is same as Parallel Toes in Footwork
Keep heels still, adductors engaged and legs parallel
Do not straighten knees completely
Keep pelvis lifted during extensions

name: Short Spine
setup: Starting in Frog Extended position, headrest down

movement.action: Straighten legs, plantarflex feet; roll up onto shoulders; articulate spine down, keep feet over eyes
Bring legs overhead keeping pelvis stable; bend knees to Frog position; dorsiflex feet, roll sacrum down to mat, move legs forward, return to start position

cues: Bring carriage all the way to stopper before rolling up
Keep tension in straps when rolling up onto shoulders
Keep thighs parallel to floor and back extensors engaged when in overhead Frog position

name: Long Spine
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, hips externally rotated, legs straight on diagonal line (45 to 60 degrees), feet in straps, plantarflexed, headrest down

movement.action: Lift legs perpendicular to floor; open legs shoulder width apart
Roll up onto shoulder girdle; roll down to sacrum, lower and close legs, return to start position

cues: Use hamstrings concentrically rolling up, eccentrically rolling down
Anchor pelvis prior to roll up and following roll down
Keep carriage still during roll up and roll down

name: Lunge Standing
setup: Standing, front leg bent in line with footbar, knee over ankle, knee of other leg placed on carriage, foot against shoulder rest, arms straight, hands placed on footbar, inside hand in line with shoulder

movement.action: Stretch hip flexors of leg on carriage (3-5 breaths); bend front leg, return to start position
Dorsiflex foot on floor, straighten leg and lower trunk forward (3-5 breaths)

cues: Bias toward posterior tilt of pelvis during hip flexor stretch
Bias toward anterior tilt of pelvis during hamstring stretch
Move pelvis along horizontal line when straightening and bending front leg

name: Lunge Kneeling
setup: Kneeling on carriage, one foot on footbar, knee over ankle, other foot against shoulder rest, knee on carriage, arms straight, hands placed on footbar, shoulder width apart

movement.action: Stretch hip flexors of leg on carriage (3-5 breaths); bend leg, return to start position
Straighten front leg, lower trunk forward (3-5 breaths)

cues: Bias toward posterior tilt of pelvis during hip flexor stretch
Bias toward anterior tilt of pelvis during hamstring stretch
Move pelvis along horizontal line when straightening and bending front leg
Maintain spinal extensor engagement during hamstring stretch

name: Lunge Forward
setup: Standing on carriage, place one foot against shoulder rest, kneel on carriage, place front foot on footbar, straighten back leg lifting knee off the carriage and pressing heel into shoulder rest, keep front knee over ankle

movement.action: Stretch hip flexors of back leg (3-5 breaths); bend front leg, return to start position
Straighten front leg, lower trunk forward (3-5 breaths)

cues: Bias toward posterior tilt of pelvis during hip flexor stretch
Bias toward anterior tilt of pelvis during hamstring stretch
Move pelvis along horizontal line when straightening and bending front leg
Maintain spinal extensor engagement during hamstring stretch

name: Scooter Round Back
setup: Standing, one foot on floor, leg bent, other foot against shoulder rest, leg bent, heel of foot on floor lined up with other foot, arms straight, hands placed on footbar, inside hand in line with shoulder, trunk in C curve position

movement.action: Straighten leg against shoulder rest (not completely)
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Maintain C curve position
Focus on hip disassociation
Keep standing leg bent and stable
Keep pelvis at consistent height

name: Scooter Flat Back
setup: Standing, one foot on floor, leg bent, other foot against shoulder rest, leg bent, heel of foot on floor lined up with other foot, arms straight, hands placed on footbar, inside hand in line with shoulder, trunk in neutral spine position

movement.action: Straighten leg against shoulder rest (not completely)
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Maintain neutral spine position
Focus on hip disassociation
Keep standing leg bent and stable
Keep pelvis at consistent height

name: Knee Stretch Round Back
setup: Kneeling on carriage with sit bones toward heels, feet against shoulder rests, arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands placed on footbar, round trunk into C curve position

movement.action: Move legs back
Move legs forward, return to start position

cues: Emphasize 'in' phase of movement
Focus on hip disassociation
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Maintain C curve position
Maintain scapular stabilization

name: Knee Stretch Flat Back
setup: Kneeling on carriage with sit bones toward heels, feet against shoulder rests, arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands placed on footbar, trunk in neutral spine position

movement.action: Move legs back
Move legs forward, return to start position

cues: Emphasize 'in' phase of movement
Focus on hip disassociation
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Maintain neutral spine position
Maintain scapular stabilization

name: Up Stretch 1
setup: Standing on carriage, legs straight, heels against shoulder rests, arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands placed on footbar, head between arms, pelvis lifted toward ceiling, back flat

movement.action: Move legs back
Move legs forward, return to start position

cues: Keep tailbone reaching upwards
Emphasize 'in' phase of movement
Focus on hip disassociation
Keep trunk and shoulders stable

name: Elephant
setup: Starting in Up Stretch 1 position, feet flat on carriage, heels against shoulder rests

movement.action: Move legs back
Move legs forward, return to start position

cues: Keep tailbone reaching upwards
Emphasize 'in' phase of movement
Focus on hip disassociation
Keep trunk and shoulders stable
Keep movement small

name: Knee Stretch Reverse
setup: Kneeling on carriage, quadruped position, knees slightly away from shoulder rests, arms straight, hands placed on F2 handles (or rails), shoulders slightly forward of hands, trunk in C curve position

movement.action: Move legs forward
Move legs back, return to start position

cues: Maintain C curve position
Focus on hip disassociation
Move knees toward chest
Engage shoulder extensors as knees move forward

name: Down Stretch
setup: Kneeling on carriage, feet against shoulder rest, arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands placed on footbar, body in arc shape

movement.action: Move body down and back
Move body up and forward, return to start position

cues: Keep abdominals engaged to avoid pressure on lumbar spine
Keep heels pressing against shoulder rests
Bias toward posterior tilt of pelvis
Maintain consistent arc shape with body
Begin and end movement with carriage against stopper

name: Stomach Massage Round Back
setup: Sitting on carriage, legs bent, toes placed on footbar, feet in V position, hands placed on front edge of carriage, trunk in C curve position

movement.action: Straighten legs, dorsiflex feet, plantarflex feet
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Maintain C curve position
Keep shoulders aligned over hips
Press hands against front of carriage
Straighten legs completely prior to dorsiflexion and plantarflexion

name: Stomach Massage Flat Back
setup: Starting in Stomach Massage Round Back position, arms bent, hands on shoulder rests, fingers facing away from body, back straight

movement.action: Straighten legs, dorsiflex feet, plantarflex feet
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Keep trunk upright and stable
Keep elbows slightly bent and reaching back
Maintain scapular stabilization (as close as possible)
Straighten legs completely prior to dorsiflexion and plantarflexion

name: Stomach Massage Reaching
setup: Starting in Stomach Massage Flat Back position, back straight, arms straight, reaching up and forward, V position, palms facing down

movement.action: Straighten legs, dorsiflex feet, plantarflex feet
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Keep trunk upright and stable
Keep arms on consistent diagonal line
Maintain scapular stabilization
Straighten legs completely prior to dorsiflexion and plantarflexion

name: Up Stretch 2
setup: Starting in Up Stretch 1 position

movement.action: Lower pelvis into Front Support position
Lift pelvis, return to start position

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Keep head aligned with spine
Pivot around hip joints and shoulder joints
Emphasize hip and shoulder disassociation
Maintain consistent angle of arms

name: Long Stretch
setup: Starting in Front Support position, heels against shoulder rests

movement.action: Move body forward
Move body back, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization
Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep head aligned with spine
Bias toward slight posterior tilt of pelvis
Move carriage forward until shoulders are over footbar

name: Extension
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs in tabletop position, arms straight and perpendicular to floor, hands in straps, palms facing forward

movement.action: Lower arms to sides of body
Lift arms, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep trunk and legs stable
Keep arms straight
Maintain tension in straps

name: Adduction
setup: Starting in Extension position with arms out to sides in T position, hands in straps, palms facing body

movement.action: Move arms to sides of body
Move arms away from body, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain micro-bend in elbows
Keep trunk and legs stable
Move arms along horizontal line, parallel to floor

name: Circles Up
setup: Starting in Adduction position

movement.action: Move arms to sides of body, rotate arms, palms facing down
Lift arms perpendicular to floor, move arms out to sides, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep trunk and legs stable
Keep movement fluid
Reach each point of the circle

name: Circles Down
setup: Starting in Extension position

movement.action: Lower arms to sides of body, rotate arms, palms face body
Open arms to T position, lift arms perpendicular to floor, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep trunk and legs stable
Keep movement fluid

name: Triceps
setup: Starting in Extension position, hands in straps, arms straight by sides of body, palms facing carriage

movement.action: Bend arms
Straighten arms, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep trunk and legs stable
Keep elbows pressing against sides of body
Keep wrists straight and stable
Keep upper arms parallel to floor and in line with shoulders

name: Chest Expansion
setup: Sitting upright at front edge of carriage, facing risers, legs straight between shoulder rests, arms straight and forward of trunk, hands in straps, palms facing back

movement.action: Move arms back
Move arms forward, return to start position

cues: Keep trunk upright and stable
Keep arms straight
Move arms 20 degrees forward and 20 degrees back of trunk

name: Biceps
setup: Starting in Chest Expansion position, arms straight and forward, shoulder height, palms facing up

movement.action: Bend arms
Straighten arms, return to start position

cues: Keep trunk upright and stable
Keep upper arms at shoulder height, parallel to floor
Straighten elbows completely following each repetition
Maintain neutral position of wrists

name: Rhomboids 1
setup: Starting in Biceps position, arms bent at 90 degrees and parallel to each other, shoulder height, forearms in straps, palms facing body

movement.action: Move arms out to sides
Move arms forward, return to start position

cues: Move upper arms parallel to floor
Bias shoulders toward external rotation
Maintain consistent position of arms with palms facing body

name: Rhomboids 2
setup: Starting in Rhomboids 1 position

movement.action: Move arms out to sides, glide scapulae together
Glide scapulae apart, move arms forward, return to start position

cues: Move upper arms parallel to floor
Bias shoulders towards external rotation
Maintain consistent position of arms with palms facing body
Continue moving arms back as scapulae adduct

name: Hug-a-Tree
setup: Sitting upright facing footbar, legs straight and together, pelvis against shoulder rests, arms in T position, micro-bend in elbows, hands in straps, palms facing forward

movement.action: Move arms forward
Move arms back, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Move arms along horizontal line at shoulder height, parallel to floor
Move arms forward until parallel with each other

name: Salute
setup: Starting in Hug-a-Tree position, arms bent, hands in straps in line with eyes, fingers facing forward

movement.action: Straighten arms forward and up on diagonal line
Bend arms, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep elbows lifted and reaching outward
Keep fingers pointing in direction of movement

name: Chest Expansion
setup: Kneeling in upright position on carriage, knees against shoulder rests, arms straight and forward of trunk, hands holding folded straps, palms facing body

movement.action: Move arms back
Move arms forward, return to start position

cues: Keep trunk upright and stable
Bias towards posterior tilt of pelvis
Keep arms straight
Move arms 20 degrees forward and 20 degrees back of trunk

name: Circles Up
setup: Kneeling in upright position on carriage, facing footbar, feet against shoulder rests, arms straight by sides of body, hands in straps, palms facing forward

movement.action: Lift arms forward and up, rotate arms, palms face forward
Circle arms out to T position, lower arms to sides of body, return to start position

cues: Keep movement fluid
Maintain upright position of trunk
Bias towards posterior tilt of pelvis
Keep arms in peripheral vision when circling around
Reach overhead position prior to rotating arms

name: Circles Down
setup: Starting in Circles Up position

movement.action: Lift arms out to sides and up, rotate arms, palms face up
Lower arms down in front of body, return to start position

cues: Keep movement fluid
Maintain upright position of trunk
Bias towards posterior tilt of pelvis
Keep arms in peripheral vision when circling around
Reach overhead position prior to rotating arms

name: Triceps
setup: Starting in Circles Up position, arms bent, elbows facing outward, hands in straps, behind head, forming triangle with thumbs and index fingers

movement.action: Straighten arms
Bend arms, return to start position

cues: Keep fingertips pointing toward ceiling
Keep elbows reaching out to sides
Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain trunk stabilization

name: Biceps
setup: Starting in Circles Up position, arms straight and back of trunk, parallel to each other (as close as possible), hands holding straps

movement.action: Bend arms
Straighten arms, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization, avoiding ribs flaring
Keep upper arms and elbows stable
Maintain neutral position of scapulae (as close as possible)
Maintain neutral position of wrists

name: Mermaid
setup: Sitting sideways on carriage, one leg bent against shoulder rests, other leg bent in front of body, arms straight, one hand placed on footbar in line with shoulder, other arm down by side

movement.action: Push carriage out, lower body until arm is parallel with floor, simultaneously lift other arm to diagonal line; rotate trunk back to previous position
Rotate trunk, bring arm around to footbar; bring carriage in, return to start position

objectives: Spinal lateral flexor and rotator control
cues: Keep both arms straight
Pivot around supporting shoulder during rotation

name: Breaststroke Prep 1
setup: Lying prone on Long Box, neutral spine position, legs straight and together, parallel to floor, feet plantarflexed, arms bent, elbows facing outward, hands on footbar

movement.action: Straighten arms
Bend arms, return to start position

cues: Maintain abdominal engagement
Maintain back and hip extensor engagement
Keep wrists firm and fingers wrapped around footbar
Face elbows outward as if arms are on flat surface

name: Breaststroke Prep 2
setup: Starting in Breaststroke Prep 1 position

movement.action: Straighten arms, simultaneously lifting trunk
Bend arms, lowering trunk, return to start position

cues: Maintain abdominal engagement
Keep wrists firm and fingers wrapped around footbar
Articulate spine into extension starting from crown of head
Face elbows outward as if arms are on flat surface

name: Breaststroke
setup: Starting in Breaststroke Prep 1 position, elbows bent by sides of body, thumbs in straps, hands in line with shoulders

movement.action: Lift trunk, straighten arms forward, circle arms around to sides of body
Bend arms, lower trunk, return to start position

cues: Keep legs parallel to floor
Initiate movement with back extensors
Maintain external rotation of shoulders as body is lowered and elbows bend during final phase of movement

name: Pulling Straps 1
setup: Lying prone on Long Box, neutral spine position, legs straight and together, parallel to floor, feet plantarflexed toward footbar, arms straight, forward of shoulders, hands holding folded straps, palms facing each other

movement.action: Lift trunk, move arms back to sides of body
Lower trunk, move arms forward, return to start position

cues: Keep palms facing each other
Initiate movement with back extensors
Articulate spine into extension starting from crown of head
Bring arms forward of shoulder line no more than 20 degrees
Keep legs parallel to floor

name: Pulling Straps 2
setup: Starting in Pulling Straps 1 position, arms in T position, parallel to floor, hands through the straps, palms facing down

movement.action: Lift trunk, move arms to sides of body
Lower trunk, move arms to T position, return to start position

cues: Move arms on horizontal line, parallel to floor
Emphasize mid and upper back extensors
Keep palms facing floor
Keep legs parallel to floor

name: Double Leg
setup: Lying supine, pelvis in front of shoulder rests (approximately 1/4 way down carriage from shoulder rests), lift legs together into tabletop position, straps around thighs above knees, chest lifted, fingers interlaced behind head, footbar down

movement.action: Straighten legs (not completely)
Bend legs

cues: Draw knees in toward forehead beyond tabletop position
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Maintain consistent height of trunk
Maintain slight tension in straps when straightening legs

name: Double Leg with Rotation
setup: Starting in Double Leg position

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, rotate trunk to one side
Straighten legs, rotate trunk to center
Bend legs, rotate trunk to other side

cues: Keep elbows wide
Maximize rotation while avoiding lateral flexion
Maintain consistent height of trunk
Maintain slight tension in straps when straightening legs

name: Backstroke
setup: Lying supine on Long Box, shoulders over back edge, chest lifted, legs in tabletop position, arms bent, elbows wide, hands in straps, fingers touching (palms facing up), hands opposite forehead and above sternum, eyes focused forward, footbar up

movement.action: Straighten arms and legs to ceiling, externally rotate hips, circle arms out to T position, simultaneously circle legs out to sides
Bring arms to sides of body simultaneously bringing legs together, bend arms and legs, internally rotating hips to parallel, return to start position

cues: Move arms and legs simultaneously
Keep eye line directly forward
Maintain maximum trunk flexion

name: Semi-Circle
setup: Resting on shoulder girdle, arms overhead, hands placed against shoulder rests, toes placed on footbar, feet in V position, pelvis lifted

movement.action: Roll down
Straighten legs (not completely)
Roll up
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Keep hip adductors engaged
Keep heels stable and squeezing together
Minimize movement of carriage when rolling down and up
Articulate through each vertebra when rolling down and up

name: Semi-Circle Reverse
setup: Starting in Semi-Circle position

movement.action: Straighten legs (not completely)
Roll down
Bend legs
Roll up, return to start position

cues: Keep hip adductors engaged
Keep heels stable and squeezing together
Minimize movement of carriage when rolling down and up
Articulate through each vertebra when rolling down and up

name: Teaser Prep
setup: Lying supine on Long Box, shoulders over back edge, legs in tabletop position, arms straight and overhead, hands placed in straps, footbar up

movement.action: Roll up, bring arms forward and up to sides of body in line with shoulders, parallel to floor, palms facing up, simultaneously straighten legs to ceiling
Roll down, take arms down and back overhead, simultaneously bend legs, return to start position

cues: Scoop arms down and forward when rolling up
Keep legs perpendicular when straightening to ceiling
Keep eye line directly forward when lifted
Keep head aligned with spine when in start position

name: Shoulder Push Double Arm
setup: Kneeling at front edge of carriage, sitting on heels, arms straight, hands placed on footbar in line with shoulders, trunk on diagonal line, neutral spine position, lower trunk to thighs, pushing carriage out

movement.action: Bend arms
Straighten arms
Lift trunk, return to start position

cues: Reach elbows out to sides as arms bend
Maintain engagement of back extensors
Keep trunk parallel to carriage
Maintain scapular stabilization

name: Shoulder Push Single Arm
setup: Starting in Shoulder Push position, one hand placed on footbar in line with shoulder, arm straight, other hand placed on front edge of carriage, trunk on diagonal line, neutral spine position, lower trunk to thighs, pushing carriage out

movement.action: Bend arm
Straighten arm
Lift trunk, return to start position

cues: Reach elbow out to side as arm bends
Maintain engagement of back extensors

name: Rowing Back 1
setup: Sitting upright on carriage facing risers, approximately 8 inches (20 cm) from front edge, legs straight between shoulder rests (crossed if necessary), footbar down, arms straight and shoulder height, hands holding straps, fist position, palms facing each other

movement.action: Bend arms, elbows out to sides, bring hands to sternum
Roll down
Straighten arms out to sides, internally rotating shoulders
Move trunk forward, arms reaching back, touch hands
Circle arms parallel to floor around to front
Roll up, return to start position

cues: Leave space behind pelvis when sitting so sacrum and lower lumbar vertebrae are supported when supine
Roll down when hands touch chest
Keep hands opposite sternum during roll down
Keep carriage still when moving arms out to sides and when moving trunk forward and arms back
Touch hands behind back when trunk is forward over legs
Keep spinal extensors engaged when circling arms around to front
Articulate spine when rolling up to sitting position, keeping arms straight and forward

name: Rowing Back 2
setup: Starting in Rowing Back 1 position, palms facing up

movement.action: Bend arms to 90 degrees
Roll down, lowering arms to sides
Pause
Lift trunk, simultaneously straighten arms diagonally forward, return to start position

cues: Avoid hyperlordosis when lifting from supine to sitting
Keep elbows at 90 degrees when rolling down
Initiate lift of trunk with spinal flexion prior to spinal extension

name: Rowing Front 1
setup: Sitting upright facing footbar, legs straight and together, pelvis against shoulder rests, thumbs in straps, fingers facing forward, elbows by sides, rope between sides of trunk and arms

movement.action: Straighten arms forward slightly above shoulder height
Lower arms, fingers touch carriage
Internally rotate shoulders, lift arms overhead, circle arms around and down to sides, palms facing forward
Bend arms, return to start position

cues: Maintain upright position of trunk
Keep arms relaxed in start position with elbows floating by sides (not pressing into trunk)
Maintain scapular stabilization
Internally rotate shoulders when lifting arms overhead so ropes pass arms
Keep hands in peripheral vision when lowering arms from overhead to sides

name: Rowing Front 2
setup: Starting in Rowing Front 1 position

movement.action: Round trunk forward, elbows by sides, forearms diagonal to carriage, hands under shoulders
Straighten trunk on diagonal line, reach arms overhead, internally rotating shoulders
Lift trunk to upright position, circle arms around and down to sides, palms facing forward
Bend arms, return to start position

cues: Keep arms relaxed in start position with elbows floating by sides (not pressing into trunk)
Initiate back extension from lumbar spine prior to straightening arms
Internally rotate shoulders when lifting arms overhead so ropes pass arms
Establish straight diagonal line with trunk and arms
Pause in diagonal line position prior to sitting upright
Keep hands in peripheral vision when lowering arms from overhead to sides

name: Deltoid Reach
setup: Kneeling sideways on carriage, legs shoulder width apart and parallel, one leg against shoulder rests, trunk upright, hand closest to shoulder rests in strap, palm facing ear, arm bent, elbow out to side

movement.action: Straighten arm toward ceiling
Bend arm, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization
Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep hand on vertical line with shoulder
Maintain slight external rotation of shoulder

name: Cross Arm Pull
setup: Starting in Deltoid Reach position, opposite hand in strap, palm facing body

movement.action: Straighten arm out to side
Bend arm, return to start position

cues: Position hand between navel and sternum in set up
Begin movement with upper arm followed by lower arm
Keep elbow higher than hand when straightening and bending arm
Maintain neutral position of wrist

name: Triceps
setup: Kneeling sideways on carriage, eyes looking toward hand on shoulder rest, legs shoulder width apart and parallel, one leg aligned with front edge of carriage, trunk in lateral flexion, bottom arm straight, hand on shoulder rest, top arm bent, elbow reaching to ceiling, hand in strap, palm facing up

movement.action: Straighten arm toward ceiling
Bend arm, return to start position

cues: Keep pressing bottom hand into shoulder rest
Keep weight evenly distributed on both legs and pelvis level

name: Arms Overhead
setup: Starting in Triceps position, arms in T position, palms facing up

movement.action: Lift arms overhead
Lower arms, return to start position

cues: Move arms symmetrically
Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain trunk stabilization, avoiding lateral flexion
Touch fingers overhead (if possible)

name: Hamstring Curl
setup: Lying prone on Long Box, facing footbar, arms hugging front of box, trunk lifted, legs straight and together, thighs lifted off box, feet in straps, ropes shortened, footbar up

movement.action: Bend legs
Straighten legs, return to start position

cues: Keep upper back lifted and chest open
Keep pressing arms into box
Keep abdominals engaged
Maintain hip extension

name: Skating Single Leg
setup: Standing upright, one foot on Foot Platform, other foot on front edge of carriage, hands loosely interlaced behind head, legs bent, weight on leg on Foot Platform

movement.action: Straighten leg on carriage
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Keep weight on supporting leg
Maintain consistent height of body
Straighten moving leg completely

name: Split Side
setup: Standing upright, feet aligned with each other, one foot on front of Foot Platform, other foot against shoulder rest, arms in T position, palms facing down

movement.action: Open legs, moving carriage out
Close legs, moving carriage in, return to start position

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Pause when carriage is fully out and when carriage is against stopper
Imagine reaching crown of head to ceiling throughout

name: Parallel Position
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs bent and parallel, feet placed on Jump Board hip width apart, heels pressing down

movement.action: Straighten legs, plantarflex feet, jump
Land, bend legs, return to start position

cues: Articulate through feet on take off and landing
Maintain heel contact with Jump Board prior to jump and following landing
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: V-Position
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, hips externally rotated, feet placed on Jump Board in small V position, heels pressing down

movement.action: Straighten legs, plantarflex feet, jump
Land, bend legs, return to start position

cues: Keep knees aligned over feet
Keep legs together during jump
Maintain heel contact with Jump Board prior to jump and following landing

name: Single Leg Parallel
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs parallel, one foot on Jump Board, heel pressing down, other leg in tabletop position

movement.action: Straighten leg, plantarflex foot, jump
Land, bend leg, return to start position

cues: Keep leg that is in tabletop position stable
Articulate through foot on take off and landing
Maintain heel contact with Jump Board prior to jump and following landing
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Leg Changes
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs parallel, one foot on Jump Board, heel pressing down, other leg in tabletop position

movement.action: Straighten leg, plantarflex foot, jump, simultaneously straighten other leg
Land on opposite foot, bend leg, alternate

cues: Straighten both legs after jump and prior to landing
Articulate through foot on take off and landing
Maintain heel contact with Jump Board prior to jump and following landing

name: Teaser
setup: Starting in Teaser 1 position, arms straight, in line with shoulders, parallel to floor, palms facing up

movement.action: Roll down, keeping legs straight and together on diagonal line
Roll up, return to start position

cues: Keep legs at consistent angle
Articulate through spine when rolling up and down
Keep eye line directly forward when lifted
Keep head aligned with spine when in start position

name: Up Stretch 3
setup: Starting in Up Stretch 1 position

movement.action: Lower pelvis into Front Support position, move carriage forward to stopper
Lift pelvis, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization
Hold carriage on stopper when transitioning from Long Stretch to Up Stretch 1
Initiate lifting pelvis from Long Stretch to Up Stretch with abdominals followed by back extensors

name: Up Stretch 3 Reverse
setup: Starting in Up Stretch 1 position

movement.action: Lower pelvis into Long Stretch position, holding carriage on stopper
Push body back to Front Support position, lift pelvis to Up Stretch 1 position, return to start position

cues: Hold carriage on stopper when transitioning from Up Stretch position to Long Stretch position
Initiate lifting pelvis from Front Support position to Up Stretch 1 position with abdominals followed by back extensors

name: Long Back Stretch
setup: Facing risers, hands placed on footbar shoulder width apart, fingers facing feet, arms straight, feet dorsiflexed against shoulder rests, trunk upright in neutral spine position

movement.action: Bend arms, lower body
Curl pelvis up, simultaneously move carriage out and straighten arms creating straight diagonal line with body, round trunk, move carriage back to stopper
Straighten back, return to start position

cues: Maximize and maintain trunk flexion when bringing carriage in

name: Tendon Stretch
setup: Facing risers, hands placed on footbar shoulder width apart, fingers facing feet, arms straight, feet dorsiflexed placed on front edge of carriage, trunk rounded

movement.action: Lower trunk, moving carriage out
Lift trunk, moving carriage back to stopper, return to start position

cues: Maximize spinal flexion
Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep head aligned with spine, eyes directed toward body
Press heels down throughout, front edge of carriage under balls of feet

name: Balance Control Front
setup: Standing on carriage facing risers, hands placed on shoulder rests, shoulders over wrists, place one foot on footbar plantarflexed, straighten leg, place other foot plantarflexed on footbar, straighten leg into Front Support position

movement.action: Flex shoulders, moving carriage out
Extend shoulders, moving carriage in towards stopper, return to start position

cues: Keep feet plantarflexed
Keep range of motion of shoulder flexion within range of scapular and trunk control
Minimize changes of body height during shoulder flexion

name: Balance Control Back Prep
setup: Sitting on carriage facing footbar, arms bent, hands placed on shoulder rests, fingers facing forward, trunk upright, legs straight, feet placed on footbar hip width apart, straighten arms, lifting body

movement.action: Extend shoulders, moving carriage out
Flex shoulders, moving carriage in towards stopper, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain neutral spine position
Hinge at shoulder and hip joints

name: Balance Control Back
setup: Starting in Balance Control Back Prep position

movement.action: Lift pelvis to Back Support position, moving carriage out
Lower pelvis, moving carriage in toward stopper, return to start position

cues: Hinge at shoulder and hip joints
Maintain co-contraction of back extensors and abdominals
Initiate movement with shoulder extensors followed immediately by hip and back extensors

name: Side Over on Box
setup: Sitting sideways on Short Box, one leg straight, foot dorsiflexed and under foot strap, other leg bent on top of box, hands behind head, body on straight diagonal line

movement.action: Arc trunk sideways, lowering trunk
Straighten and lift trunk, return to start position
Arc trunk sideways, lowering trunk
Straighten and lift trunk, return to start position

cues: Maintain hip of straight leg in parallel position
Maintain co-contraction of abdominals and back extensors
Touch fingers loosely behind head
Imagine moving between two panes of glass
